The file appears to be a structured listing, but formatting in the sample is noisy, so some column boundaries or values may be uncertain.

result.preview_text:
Van Gosen, Bradley S.
Johnson, Michaela R.
Goldman, Margaret A.
20161031
Three GIS datasets defining areas permissive for the occurrence of uranium-bearing, solution-collapse breccia pipes in northern Arizona and southeast Utah
Vector Digital Data Sets (Polygon and Point)
Denver, CO
U.S. Geological Survey
https://dx.doi.org/10.5066/F76D5R3Z
Some of the highest grade uranium (U) deposits in the United States are hosted by solution-collapse breccia pipes in the Grand Canyon region of northern Arizona. These structures are named for their vertical, pipe-like shape and the broken rock (breccia) that fills them. Hundreds, perhaps thousands, of these structures exist. Not all of the breccia pipes are mineralized; only a small percentage of the identified breccia pipes are known to contain an economic uranium deposit. An unresolved question is how many undiscovered U-bearing breccia pipes of this type exist in northern Arizona, in the region sometimes referred to as the “Arizona Strip”. Two principal questions remain regarding the breccia pipe U deposits of northern Arizona are: (1) What processes combined to form these unusual structures and their U deposits? and (2) How many undiscovered U deposits hosted by breccia pipes exist in the region? A piece of information required to answer these questions is to define the area where these types of deposits could exist based on available geologic information. In order to determine the regional processes that led to their formation, the regional distribution of U-bearing breccia pipes must be considered. These geospatial datasets were assembled in support of this goal.
Data were collected to (1) determine the distribution of uranium deposits, breccia pipes, and collapse features and (2) define areas permissible for uranium-bearing breccia pipes in northern Arizona and southeast Utah.
This USGS Data Release consists of 2 point and 1 polygon shapefiles.  
The point shapefile "Breccia_pipes_Uranium_NArizona_SEUtah.shp" provides locations for 64 breccia pipes in the Grand Canyon region of northern Arizona that are known to contain U mineralization.  
The point shapefile, Permissive_structures_Uranium_NArizona_SEUtah.shp, contains "breccia_pipe" (54 additional mapped breccia pipes within permissive areas; some of these breccia pipes may host U deposits, but they have not been drilled at this time) and "collapse_structures" (748 mapped collapse features; these features may be the surface expression of breccia pipes, and thus some may overlie a U-bearing breccia pipe). 
The polygon shapefile, Permissive_areas_Uranium_NArizona_SEUtah.shp, contains "Permissive", "Surficial deposits", and "Volcanic rocks" constructed from shapefiles provided in nine published 1:24,000-, 1:100,000-, and 1:500,000-scale geologic maps that cover the Grand Canyon region.
2016
publication date
Complete
Not planned
-114.038841184

profile.llm_description: Text metadata describing a USGS geospatial data release: three GIS vector datasets (polygon and point) defining areas permissive for uranium-bearing solution-collapse breccia pipes in northern Arizona and southeast Utah. The record cites authors (Van Gosen, Johnson, Goldman), USGS publication details, and a DOI link. It provides background on breccia-pipe uranium deposits in the Grand Canyon region and notes uncertainty about how many undiscovered mineralized pipes may exist (Arizona Strip). The sample appears to be extracted citation/abstract text, so some fields may be incomplete or noisy.

profile.llm_description: Tab-delimited text file defining a 2020 semantic map (hierarchical topic taxonomy) used in the ISA literature screening dataset. Columns include year, multiple hierarchy level identifiers (numeric and character), and topical labels such as chapter category, topic, and discipline. Sample entries cover atmospheric science themes (e.g., atmospheric chemistry, meteorology, ozone metrics, background ozone definitions) organized across up to six levels. The provided sample appears header-like and repetitive, so exact delimiter/format details may require validation against the full file.

profile.llm_description: Text table of budget line items for a Proposed FY 2016 budget dataset. Rows are organized by Department, Division, Fund, and Object Code (with names), and include financial amounts for FY 2013 Actual, FY 2014 Actual, FY 2015 Budget, FY 2016 Current Services, FY 2016 Budget Issues, and FY 2016 Proposed Budget. Sample entries include Budget and Fiscal Services (OP_BFS/BFS0300C) under the General Fund with compensation-related object codes (e.g., regular pay, overtime, vacancy cutback). Formatting suggests a delimited export; some fields appear repeated or uneven, so minor parsing uncertainty may exist.
